text: Philosopher Drew Leder, in The Absent Body, observes that the healthy body tends to disappear from awareness during skilled engagement. A pianist at peak performance does not attend to fingers but through them to the music. Leder calls this 'bodily disappearance' — the body becomes transparent precisely when it is functioning well. But disruption, pain, or the glare of a smartphone notification causes the body to 'dys-appear': it retrudes into awareness as an obstacle. Reclaiming attention after digital interruption is therefore not merely a cognitive task; it requires the body to re-achieve transparency — to settle back into the absorbed, self-effacing posture that sustains focused perception.
question: What does Leder call the phenomenon where the body retreats from awareness during skilled engagement?
